critical_unresolved.title: Tawhid as objective definition vs. Tawhid requiring faith
Religion = CFS uniqueness vs. Austrian School completeness
Gödel transfers to social systems vs. Gödel is about arithmetic
Mysticism as catch-all vs. mysticism as specific phenomenon
Theory has primacy over data vs. theory must be falsifiable

candidates.title: Secular derivation vs. religious requirement
candidates.claim_a: Free will is the axiom everyone is forced to presuppose — derivable by reason alone.
candidates.claim_b: Liberty → property rights → religion (the chain terminates in religion, not just reason).
candidates.conflict: If free will and reason alone are sufficient to derive liberty, then religion is optional. But the master chain says liberty ultimately requires religion. These cannot both be strictly true.
candidates.resolution_candidate: The author's position: reason alone derives liberty up to a stability threshold; beyond that threshold (last-round problem), religion is required. This is a scope-conditional resolution, not a true contradiction.

candidates.title: No compulsion in religion vs. Islamic Republic enforcement
candidates.claim_a: No compulsion in religion — forcing religion on anyone is wrong.
candidates.claim_b: Religion is the antithesis of the state — yet the author endorses Shi'ism as Iran's protective substrate.
candidates.conflict: If religion cannot be compelled and is the antithesis of the state, then Shi'ism-as-state-religion is impossible to endorse. The author endorses Shi'ism's civilizational role while opposing the Islamic Republic — this distinction is load-bearing but not always maintained.
candidates.resolution_candidate: Shi'ism as civilizational substrate ≠ Shi'ism as state religion. The author consistently opposes institutional Velayat-e Faqih while supporting Shi'a values. Resolvable by scope.

candidates.title: Tawhid as objective definition vs. Tawhid requiring faith
candidates.claim_a: Tawhid has an objective, agreement-admissible definition (God is not any observable entity).
candidates.claim_b: The negative ostensive definition of God is sufficient for formal work — but this is itself an unproven assumption (HA-011).
candidates.conflict: The author claims Tawhid is agreement-admissible without faith. But whether 'negative ostensive definition suffices for formal axiomatic work' is itself a hidden axiom (HA-011) requiring faith in a specific theory of meaning.
candidates.resolution_candidate: Requires formal demonstration that negative ostensive definitions are sufficient for formal axiomatic systems. This is a non-trivial claim in philosophy of language.

candidates.title: Religion = CFS uniqueness vs. Austrian School completeness
candidates.claim_a: Theory of liberty is the only existing solution for AI ethics.
candidates.claim_b: Austrian School is incomplete without theological grounding.
candidates.conflict: If Austrian School is merely incomplete (not wrong), then completing it with theological grounding might produce a different CFS for liberty that is not Islam-specific. This undermines the uniqueness claim.
candidates.resolution_candidate: The author's position: Austrian School's incompleteness is precisely the last-round problem gap, and only Resurrection/Tawhid/Prophethood fill it. This is a strong claim requiring formal demonstration.

candidates.title: Democracy as path to communism vs. democracy as pre-liberal safeguard
candidates.claim_a: Democracy does not legitimize violating individual rights; it is the cheapest path to communism.
candidates.claim_b: The author acknowledges that liberal democracies have partially preserved liberty through the past century.
candidates.conflict: If democracy is necessarily the path to communism, why have liberal democracies (USA, UK) sustained liberty longer than non-democratic systems? The author attributes this to 'residual religious strands of liberalism' — but this is an ad hoc qualification.
candidates.resolution_candidate: The author argues democracy buys time but does not solve the terminal problem. This is a temporal scope qualification: democracy delays but does not prevent the slide. Partially resolvable.

candidates.title: Gödel transfers to social systems vs. Gödel is about arithmetic
candidates.claim_a: A valid system must be internally consistent (Gödel) — applied to religion as CFS.
candidates.claim_b: Gödel's theorems apply to formal systems of sufficient arithmetic power (hidden axiom HA-008 denies this transfer is proven).
candidates.conflict: The theory's use of 'Consistent' (Gödel-sense) for religion requires that religion's axiom system meets the complexity threshold for Gödel's theorems. Whether it does is a technical question in formal logic, not settled by the author.
candidates.resolution_candidate: The author may intend 'consistent' in the ordinary sense (non-contradictory) rather than the Gödel-technical sense. If so, the Gödel reference is illustrative, not load-bearing. This must be clarified before formal analysis.

candidates.title: Mysticism as catch-all vs. mysticism as specific phenomenon
candidates.claim_a: Mysticism is the philosophical pipeline from anti-property to totalitarianism.
candidates.claim_b: Hegel, Marx, fascism, communism are recyclings of ancient mysticism.
candidates.conflict: If mysticism is defined as 'any anti-liberty system,' then C-000074 becomes true by definition: anything anti-liberty is mysticism, therefore anti-liberty ideologies are mysticism. This creates a circular argument.
candidates.resolution_candidate: Must give mysticism an independent characterization (structural: separates person from reason/property) and then prove that Hegel etc. exhibit this structural feature. Cannot use liberty-violation in the definition.

candidates.title: Atheist liberalism as delayed communism vs. classical liberals as allies
candidates.claim_a: Atheist liberals are delayed communists (they will eventually reintroduce servitude).
candidates.claim_b: Austrian School (largely atheist or agnostic in its founders) is incomplete but not wrong — it is the closest secular theory to the truth.
candidates.conflict: Mises, Hayek, Rothbard — all non-theists — produced the closest secular approximation of the author's theory. If atheist liberals are necessarily delayed communists, how can their work be the second-best approximation of the theory?
candidates.resolution_candidate: The author distinguishes between individual atheist liberals (whose personal journey may not complete) and their theoretical contributions (which capture part of the truth). Resolvable by distinguishing person from theory.

candidates.title: Law is discovered vs. law requires axiomatic specification
candidates.claim_a: Law is discovered, not enacted — natural law principle.
candidates.claim_b: A consistent formal system is necessary for understanding religion/liberty — law must be formally specified.
candidates.conflict: Natural law discovery and formal axiomatic specification are different epistemological frameworks. Discovery implies intuition and empirical observation; formal axiomatization implies explicit deductive construction. The theory uses both without clearly reconciling them.
candidates.resolution_candidate: The author may mean: law is ontologically discovered (it exists independently of human will) but epistemologically requires axiomatic specification to be reliably communicated. The tension is real but resolvable.

candidates.title: Theory has primacy over data vs. theory must be falsifiable
candidates.claim_a: Theory has primacy over data in understanding history and religion.
candidates.claim_b: The theory makes empirical claims (Iran, Ghadir, game theory results) that it treats as confirmations of the theory.
candidates.conflict: If theory has primacy over data, empirical evidence cannot confirm or falsify the theory — only illustrate it. But the author uses historical and economic evidence as supporting the theory. This is methodologically inconsistent: evidence cannot both be subordinate to theory and serve as its confirmation.
candidates.resolution_candidate: The author's methodology may be: theory selects which data to look at, but data within that frame still serves as evidence. This is consistent with the Lakatosian hard-core / protective-belt model. Requires explicit methodological statement.
